classes.name: aporacle.runnables.runnable_base.RunnableBase

methods.name: aporacle.runnables.runnable_base.RunnableBase.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a new instance of the SmartComponentBase class.

```
param update_interval

The interval at which the control loop should be executed, in seconds.
```

methods.name: aporacle.runnables.runnable_base.RunnableBase.status
documentation:
Get the current status of the smart component.

```
return

The current status of the smart component.
```

methods.name: aporacle.runnables.runnable_base.RunnableBase.start
documentation:
Start the control loop of the smart component.
If the component is not already started, it will start the control loop.

methods.name: aporacle.runnables.runnable_base.RunnableBase.stop
documentation:
Stop the control loop of the smart component.
If the component is active or not started, it will stop the control loop.

methods.name: aporacle.runnables.runnable_base.RunnableBase.control_loop
documentation:
The main control loop of the smart component.
This method is responsible for executing the control task at the specified interval.

methods.name: aporacle.runnables.runnable_base.RunnableBase.on_stop
documentation:
Method to be executed when the control loop is stopped.
This method should be overridden in subclasses to provide specific behavior.

methods.name: aporacle.runnables.runnable_base.RunnableBase.on_start
documentation:
Method to be executed when the control loop is started.
This method should be overridden in subclasses to provide specific behavior.

methods.name: aporacle.runnables.runnable_base.RunnableBase.control_task
documentation:
The main task to be executed in the control loop.
This method should be overridden in subclasses to provide specific behavior.

Base class for smart components in the application.
This class provides a basic structure for components that need to perform tasks at regular intervals.

functions.name: aporacle.data.gcp.list_buckets
documentation: Lists all buckets in your GCP project.

functions.name: aporacle.data.gcp.create_bucket
documentation:
Creates a bucket in Google Cloud Storage.

Args:
  bucket_name: The name of the bucket to create.
  location: The location to store the bucket data (optional). Defaults to None (US multi-region).

Returns:
  The newly created bucket object, or None on error.
1. Create bucket if not exist. Map bucket to feed.

functions.name: aporacle.data.gcp.list_bucket_files
documentation:
Lists all files (blobs) within a bucket in Google Cloud Storage.

Args:
    bucket_name: The name of the bucket to list files from.

functions.name: aporacle.data.gcp.upload_file_to_bucket
documentation:
Uploads a file to GCP storage.

Args:
  bucket_name: The name of the bucket to upload the file to.
  source_file_path: The local path of the file to upload.
  destination_blob_name: The name of the file in the bucket (optional).
3. Upload file to bucket by feed.

functions.name: aporacle.data.gcp.download_file_from_bucket
documentation:
Downloads a file from GCP storage and returns the local path.

Args:
    bucket_name: The name of the bucket containing the file.
    object_name: The name of the file to download.
    local_path: The local path to save the downloaded file.

Returns:
    The local path of the downloaded file, or None on error.
4. Download file from bucket by file_name name.

functions.name: aporacle.data.gcp.delete_file_from_bucket
documentation:
Deletes a file (blob) from a bucket in Google Cloud Storage.

Args:
    bucket_name: The name of the bucket containing the file.
    object_name: The name of the file (blob) to delete.

Returns:
    True on successful deletion, False otherwise.
5. Delete file from bucket by file_name name.

functions.name: aporacle.data.gcp.delete_bucket
documentation:
Deletes a bucket in Google Cloud Storage.

Args:
  bucket_name: The name of the bucket to delete.

Returns:
  True on successful deletion, False otherwise.
6. Delete bucket by feed or bucket name.

functions.name: aporacle.data.gcp.download_csv_from_gcp_return_df
documentation: 8. Download CSV from GCP and return as DataFrame

functions.name: aporacle.data.gcp.upload_pickle_to_gcs
documentation:
Uploads pickle data from memory to a file in GCP storage.

Args:
    bucket_name: The name of the bucket to upload the file to.
    object_name: The name of the file in the bucket.
    pickle_data: The pickled data object in memory.
9. Upload a pickled data to GCP bucket

functions.name: aporacle.data.gcp.download_and_load_pickle
documentation:
Downloads a pickle file from GCP storage and loads the data.

Args:
    bucket_name: The name of the bucket containing the file.
    object_name: The name of the pickle file to download.

Returns:
    The loaded data from the pickle file, or None on error.
10. Download a pickled data from GCP bucket and convert to sklearn Ridge models

functions.name: aporacle.data.gcp.upload_df_to_bigquery_gbq
documentation:
Uploads a pandas DataFrame to a BigQuery table using pandas-gbq.

Args:
    project_id: Your GCP project ID.
    dataset_id: The ID of the BigQuery dataset to upload to.
    table_name: The name of the BigQuery table to create or update.
    dataframe: The pandas DataFrame to upload.
    credentials_path: Path to a service account credentials file (optional).
11. Upload a dataframe to GCP BigQuery

functions.name: aporacle.data.gcp.download_bigquery_data_to_dataframe
documentation:
Downloads data from BigQuery to a pandas DataFrame using pandas-gbq.

Args:
    project_id: Your GCP project ID.
    dataset_id: The ID of the BigQuery dataset to query.
    table_name: The name of the BigQuery table to query (optional if using a query).
    query: A SQL query to retrieve data (optional, replaces table download).
    credentials_path: Path to a service account credentials file (optional).

Returns:
    A pandas DataFrame containing the downloaded data, or None on error.

functions.name: aporacle.data.db.sql.models.feed.generate_code
documentation:
Generate a sequential code for a Feed instance before inserting it into the database.
Codes are generated as 100000, 110000, 120000, ...

class_variables.name: aporacle.data.db.sql.models.evaluated_target.EvaluatedTarget.correction_factor
documentation: Correction Factor. Typically, the last observed Moving Average.

functions.name: aporacle.data.db.sql.models.symbol.generate_symbol_code
documentation:
Generate a sequential symbol code that includes the Feed code as a prefix.
If a specified feed does not exist, create the feed automatically.

E.g., if a Feed has code `100000`, the first symbol under this feed will be `100010`,
the second will be `100020`, and so on.

functions.name: aporacle.data.db.sql.models.models.generate_code
documentation:
Generate a sequential code for a Feed instance before inserting it into the database.
Codes are generated as 100000, 110000, 120000, ...

functions.name: aporacle.data.db.sql.models.models.generate_symbol_code
documentation:
Generate a sequential symbol code that includes the Feed code as a prefix.
If a specified feed does not exist, create the feed automatically.

E.g., if a Feed has code `100000`, the first symbol under this feed will be `100010`,
the second will be `100020`, and so on.

class_variables.name: aporacle.data.db.sql.models.models.EvaluatedTarget.correction_factor
documentation: Correction Factor. Typically, the last observed Moving Average.
